Algeria.text: Algeria's group stage exit from Group J ended with the kind of drama that will linger: Mahrez's 90th-minute equaliser looked to have rescued a point against Austria, only for Kalajdzic to restore the lead three minutes into stoppage time — a 3-3 thriller that rewarded neither side with composure. The Fennec Foxes nonetheless scrape through as one of the best third-placed teams, and Switzerland await in the Round of 32 on Thu Jul 2 20:00 PT — a disciplined, well-organised European side likely to enter as modest favourites against an Algeria team that has shown attacking spark but defensive fragility. Survive that, and Colombia or Ghana could follow in the Round of 16, before a potential quarterfinal confrontation with Argentina that would represent a genuine statement for North African football.

Argentina.text: Argentina closed the group stage in the manner of champions — a controlled 3-1 victory over Jordan, two first-half goals putting the tie to bed before a Messi flourish in the 80th minute rendered Jordan's brief moment of hope irrelevant. Cape Verde await in the Round of 32 on Fri Jul 3 15:00 PT, a fixture that, on paper, demands competence rather than inspiration from the world champions; the Africans are capable of disruption but this is Argentina's tie to lose. Should they advance as expected, the bracket opens toward potentially bruising quarterfinal tests against Colombia or Switzerland, and a semifinal that could produce a renewal of hostilities with Brazil or England.

Australia.text: Australia ground out a goalless draw with Paraguay to close the group stage — a functional, if unspectacular, result that secured second place in Group D without ever threatening to ignite. The Socceroos now face Egypt in the Round of 32 on Fri Jul 3 11:00 PT, a tie Australia will enter as moderate favourites, though Egypt arrive as a disciplined, physical side capable of making life uncomfortable if Australia cannot find the tempo they have so far struggled to sustain. Survive that, and Argentina could await in the Round of 16 — a collision that would represent a genuine examination of this squad's ceiling. Deeper still, Brazil or England loom as possible semifinal opponents, which lends the bracket a daunting quality that Australia must prefer not to dwell on just yet.

Austria.text:
Austria's Round of 32 exit was as emphatic as the scoreline suggests. Spain controlled proceedings from the half-hour mark, Oyarzabal striking either side of a Porro goal to complete a composed, professional dismantling — never a contest that truly threatened to turn.

Austria depart having qualified from Group J in second place, only to find Spain an entirely different proposition. Their tournament ends in the last 32, undone by a side operating at a level they could not approach.

Belgium.text: Belgium survived a harrowing afternoon against Senegal, trailing 2-0 after the 51st minute before Lukaku pulled one back in the 86th and Tielemans levelled seconds later, then sealed the comeback from the penalty spot deep into extra time — as dramatic a rescue act as the Round of 32 produced. They meet the USA in the Round of 16 on Mon Jul 6 17:00 PT, a fixture Belgium enter as clear favourites despite the hosts' home-tournament momentum and the roar of a partisan crowd behind them. Survive that, and either Portugal or Spain likely awaits in the quarterfinal — a sterner examination of whether this Belgian generation can finally go the distance.

Bosnia & Herzegovina.text: Bosnia & Herzegovina's tournament ended with a flat 0-2 defeat to hosts USA in the Round of 32, a result that flattered neither side's ambitions — the Dragons offered little going forward and were punished either side of a half-time goal from Balogun and a late Tillman strike that killed the tie. They had reached the knockout stage as one of the best third-placed teams from Group B, scrapping through on four points after a mixed group campaign, and that survival instinct ultimately ran dry against a USA side playing in front of its own crowd.

Brazil.text: Brazil edged past Japan in the Round of 32, but it was far from comfortable — Sano's 29th-minute opener meant the Seleção spent the better part of an hour chasing the game before Casemiro levelled and Martinelli rescued the win deep into stoppage time. Norway arrive next in the Round of 16 on Sun Jul 5 13:00 PT, and while Brazil enter as clear favourites, the Norwegians are physical, well-organised, and unlikely to capitulate quietly. Survive that, and a quarterfinal meeting with England or Mexico looms on Sat Jul 11 14:00 PT — either would represent a genuine examination of Brazil's credentials, with the winner potentially navigating a semifinal path that could yet feature Argentina.

Canada.text: Canada scraped through the Round of 32 on a stoppage-time winner from Stephen Eustáquio, a nervy, attritional affair against South Africa settled only in the second minute of added time. Morocco await in the Round of 16 on Sat Jul 4 10:00 PT — a genuine test of Canada's mettle, with the Atlas Lions widely regarded as the superior side and capable of ending this tournament run without breaking much of a sweat. Survive that, and France or Paraguay could stand in the quarterfinal way, with the possibility of a heavyweight collision against Belgium, Portugal, Spain, or the host nation USA looming in the semifinal.

Cape Verde.text: Cape Verde ground out a third successive draw to close the group stage, a goalless stalemate with Saudi Arabia confirming a place in the last 32 without ever quite catching fire — three points from three draws tells its own story of a side built on defensive discipline rather than adventure. That compact structure will be tested to its absolute limit on Fri Jul 3 15:00 PT when Argentina arrive in the Round of 32, a tie that represents one of the tournament's starkest mismatches on paper; the Blue Sharks carry a puncher's chance, but the world champions are overwhelming favourites. Survive that, and Australia or Egypt await in the Round of 16, before the draw opens toward the quarterfinal, where Colombia or Switzerland could provide the kind of mid-tier test Cape Verde might genuinely fancy.

Colombia.text: Colombia closed out the group stage with a goalless draw against Portugal — a measured, disciplined performance that secured top spot in Group K without conceding an inch. They meet Ghana in the Round of 32 on Fri Jul 3 18:30 PT, a fixture that reads as a manageable assignment for a Colombian side carrying genuine momentum, even if the West Africans are capable of disruption on their day. Win that, and a Round of 16 date with Algeria or Switzerland awaits before the draw opens into more formidable territory — potentially Argentina or Brazil lurking in the quarters and beyond.

Croatia.text: Croatia led Portugal past the hour mark after Ivan Perisic's second-half strike, only for a Ronaldo penalty to level matters and a stoppage-time Ramos goal to end their tournament in cruel fashion. The Golden Generation's long goodbye continues its slow fade — Zlatko Dalic's side showed enough fight to trouble one of Europe's heavyweights, but the margin, and the manner, was ultimately decisive. Their World Cup 2026 campaign ends at the Round of 32.

Curaçao.text: Curaçao's group stage ended with a 0-2 defeat to Ivory Coast, Nicolas Pépé doing the damage with goals either side of half-time — the first arriving inside seven minutes to set a tone the Curaçao defence never recovered from. They finished fourth in Group E with a single point and a goal difference of minus nine, yet still advanced to the Round of 32 as one of the tournament's best third-placed sides. There, their journey came to an abrupt end, the gulf in quality across the group stage ultimately a true reflection of where this Curaçao side stands on the world stage.

Czech Republic.text: Czech Republic's group stage ended with a thumping 3-0 defeat to Mexico, the goals arriving in a clinical second-half burst and a stoppage-time finish that underlined the gulf in class — a deflating exit for a side that managed just one point across three matches. Having finished fourth in Group A and crept through as one of the best third-placed teams, they were nonetheless unable to sustain that precarious foothold, and Mexico's composed, purposeful display exposed their limitations emphatically. The Czechs depart at the earliest opportunity, their tournament leaving little to build on.

DR Congo.text: DR Congo led England for the better part of an hour in the Round of 32, Cipenga's seventh-minute opener giving them every reason to believe before Harry Kane dismantled the dream with two clinical finishes in the final quarter-hour. It was a cruel, late unraveling — the Leopards had defended resolutely and threatened a genuine upset — only for England's captain to remind everyone why tournament football rewards patience and predatory quality. DR Congo exit at the last-32 stage, their campaign ultimately defined by the agony of a lead surrendered too late to recover.

Ecuador.text: Ecuador's exit arrived without much ceremony — a 0-2 defeat to Mexico in the Round of 32 in which they never truly threatened, conceding twice before half-time through Quiñones and a Jiménez strike deep in first-half stoppage time. The pattern was emphatic: Ecuador chasing the game from the half-hour mark, unable to find a response across 90 minutes. They had navigated the group stage only as one of the better third-placed sides, and against a Mexican outfit with genuine tournament ambition on home soil, that fragile foundation gave way entirely.

Egypt.text: Egypt finished their group stage with a frenetic but ultimately inconclusive affair against Iran — Saber struck inside five minutes only for Rezaeian to level before the quarter-hour, and neither side could find a winner, leaving the Pharaohs to advance as Group G runners-up. They face Australia in the Round of 32 on Fri Jul 3 11:00 PT, a winnable but not trivial assignment — the Socceroos carry genuine pedigree and will not simply yield, though Egypt's compact, counter-punching style gives them every reason for confidence. Survive that, and Argentina could loom in the Round of 16, with Brazil or England potentially waiting in the semifinals — a path that would demand Egypt produce something considerably more authoritative than anything they have shown so far.

England.text: England survived a nervy afternoon against DR Congo, going behind to an early Cipenga strike before Harry Kane dragged them through with two goals in the final quarter-hour — the kind of rescue act that flattered the scoreline. Mexico await in the Round of 16 on Sun Jul 5 17:00 PT, a fixture that carries genuine menace: a passionate, well-organised side on home soil who will fancy their chances against an England team that still looks fragile in the early stages of matches. Win that, and Brazil or Norway could stand in the quarterfinal, before a potential semifinal that opens onto a final against any one of France, Spain, or Portugal — a gauntlet that would test the very best.

France.text: France brushed Sweden aside with something close to contempt in the Round of 32, Mbappé scoring either side of half-time and Barcola adding a third to make it 3-0 — a performance that was controlled, clinical, and largely unhurried. Paraguay await in the Round of 16 on Sat Jul 4 14:00 PT, and few neutrals will expect Deschamps's side to break sweat; Les Bleus arrive as heavy favourites against a South American side that will defend deep and hope for a moment of fortune. Should France advance through a quarterfinal against Canada or Morocco, the semifinal draw could serve up Belgium, Portugal, Spain, or the host nation United States — any of which would constitute a genuine examination of whether this squad has the teeth to go all the way.

Germany.text: Germany's Round of 32 exit is as brutal as the format can produce: Havertz's second-half equaliser cancelled out Enciso's opener, and when neither side could be separated across 90 minutes, Paraguay held their nerve in the shootout, converting four of four while Germany stumbled to three. A group-stage winner undone before the last sixteen, Joachim Löw's side — or whoever currently stewards this generation — departs a tournament they entered with genuine ambitions, beaten not by a superior team across the pitch but by the cold lottery of penalties.

Ghana.text: Ghana limped out of the group stage on a sour note, conceding a late Vlasic goal to lose 2-1 to Croatia — a result that leaves them advancing as a third-place qualifier despite the defeat, having equalised through Luckassen just ten minutes before the sucker punch. Colombia await in the Round of 32 on Fri Jul 3 18:30 PT, a formidable assignment against a South American side that will start as clear favourites, making this something close to a free swing for Ghana. Survive that, and Algeria or Switzerland loom in the Round of 16, with Argentina a possible reckoning in the quarterfinal should the Black Stars find a way to keep their tournament alive.

Haiti.text: Haiti depart the 2026 World Cup without a point to their name, their group stage exit confirmed by a 4-2 defeat to Morocco that was comfortable for the Atlas Lions despite a spirited Haitian showing either side of half-time. Two goals inside the first 43 minutes gave Haiti brief cause for optimism, but Morocco reasserted themselves emphatically in the closing stages, scoring four times in total to send their opponents home. The Caribbeans finish bottom of Group C, undone by a gulf in quality that proved too wide to bridge across three matches.

Iran.text: Iran's group-stage finale against Egypt ended 1-1, a Rezaeian equaliser on 14 minutes cancelling out Saber's early opener — a pattern that defined their entire tournament: always in the contest, never quite through it. Three draws, no wins, and three points were enough to sneak through as one of the best third-placed sides, but the knockout round delivered the reckoning that the group stage had deferred. Iran exit the 2026 World Cup without a victory to their name, their campaign a study in stubborn resilience that ultimately fell short of the cutting edge a deep run demands.

Iraq.text: Iraq's group stage ended in humiliation against Senegal, a 5-0 defeat that was effectively settled inside the opening minutes and then ruthlessly extended across the second half, with Gueye alone scoring twice in a 12-minute spell. Finishing fourth in Group I with three losses, no points, and twelve goals conceded, Iraq's tournament was over at the earliest stage — a chastening introduction to a World Cup where the gulf in class between the continent's elite and the tournament's newcomers was laid painfully bare.

Ivory Coast.text: Ivory Coast gave Norway a genuine contest in the Round of 32, pulling level through Diallo on 74 minutes after falling behind to Nusa's first-half opener — only for Haaland to settle it with four minutes remaining, a late hammer blow that extinguished the comeback. The Elephants depart a tournament they had navigated with enough promise to suggest they belonged, yet ultimately could not withstand Norway's cutting edge when it mattered most.

Japan.text: Japan held their own against Brazil for the better part of an hour, Sano's 29th-minute opener giving them genuine belief before Casemiro levelled midway through the second half. It was Martinelli's stoppage-time winner that delivered the cruellest of exits — a Samurai Blue side that had navigated Group F without defeat ultimately undone in the final seconds of a Round of 32 tie they had come agonisingly close to taking further.

Jordan.text: Jordan's group stage ended with a 3-1 defeat to Argentina — a result that followed the script almost from the first whistle, with the South Americans establishing a two-goal cushion inside half an hour before Messi put the contest beyond doubt late on. Al-Tamari's consolation just after the hour offered a brief flicker, but Jordan were outclassed. Finishing fourth in Group J with three defeats and no points, they nevertheless advanced as one of the tournament's best third-placed sides before their World Cup came to a close.

Mexico.text: Mexico swept Ecuador aside with a composed, professional performance in the Round of 32, goals from Quiñones and Jiménez — the second arriving just before half-time to put any doubt to rest — completing a clean sheet that capped a flawless group stage. England await in the Round of 16 on Sun Jul 5 17:00 PT, a tie that represents a genuine test: whatever momentum El Tri carry into it, Gareth Southgate's side command respect as one of the tournament's heavyweight contenders, and this is nobody's routine assignment. Survive that, and Brazil or Norway could stand in the quarterfinal, with the likes of Argentina or France lurking as possible semifinal opponents deeper in the draw.

Morocco.text: Morocco's Round of 32 exit from Group C's second place was anything but comfortable against the Netherlands — Gakpo put the Dutch ahead on 72 minutes before Diop's equaliser deep in stoppage time forced penalties, where Morocco held their nerve to win 3-2 in a breathless conclusion. They now face Canada in the Round of 16 on Sat Jul 4 10:00 PT, a fixture that carries the look of a favourable draw on paper, though the host nation will arrive with the crowd and momentum of a tournament they are genuinely invested in. Survive that, and either France or Paraguay await in the quarterfinal — the prospect of a heavyweight collision with Les Bleus would represent the tournament's sternest test of Walid Regragui's side.

Netherlands.text: Netherlands dominated their group with seven points and a healthy goal difference, only to suffer one of the tournament's crueller exits — Gakpo's 72nd-minute strike cancelled out by a Moroccan equaliser deep in stoppage time, before the Dutch lost the subsequent shootout 2-3. It was the worst kind of departure: a lead surrendered at the death, then the lottery of penalties delivering the final blow. A side that had looked like genuine contenders through the group stage goes home from the Round of 32, undone in the space of twenty minutes.

New Zealand.text: Belgium administered a thorough lesson in the final group game, with Trossard's brace and further goals from De Bruyne and Lukaku turning what might have been a contest into a rout — New Zealand's consolation arriving far too late, in the 84th minute, to suggest any real foothold. The All Whites finished fourth in Group G with a single point and a goals difference of minus ten, their tournament record a frank reflection of the gulf between them and the competition's established forces. They depart a World Cup that offered little comfort beyond the experience itself.

Norway.text: Norway edged past Ivory Coast in a nervy Round of 32 encounter, Nusa's first-half opener holding until Diallo drew the Ivorians level on 74 minutes, before Haaland settled matters with a late winner four minutes from time. Next up is Brazil in the Round of 16 on Sun Jul 5 13:00 PT — a formidable assignment by any measure, with the Seleção the clear favorites and Norway cast as dangerous but outgunned underdogs. Survive that, and England or Mexico could await in the quarterfinal, with the prospect of Argentina or France lurking deeper still.

Panama.text: Panama's group stage exit was as comprehensive as the record suggests — three defeats, no goals scored, and a closing loss to England that was settled efficiently in a five-minute spell midway through the second half, Bellingham and Kane putting the game to bed without drama or late anxiety. They finish bottom of Group L with nothing to show from their three outings, and whatever hope they carried into the tournament evaporated long before the knockout rounds began. A familiar story for Central American football at this level: the gap between qualifying and competing proved too wide to bridge.

Paraguay.text: Paraguay's passage through the Round of 32 was nothing short of extraordinary — Enciso's first-half strike gave them the lead against Germany, Havertz equalised nine minutes after the break, and the Albirroja held their nerve through 90 minutes and beyond to prevail 4-3 on penalties. Now they face France in the Round of 16 on Sat Jul 4 14:00 PT, a tie in which Didier Deschamps's side will be heavy favourites — yet Paraguay arrive with fresh momentum and the psychological armour of having just dispatched a tournament giant. Should they conjure another upset, Canada or Morocco await in the quarterfinal, with the prospect of Spain, Portugal, or Belgium looming in the semifinal beyond that.

Portugal.text: Portugal squeezed past Croatia in a Round of 32 tie that looked to be slipping away from them — Perisic's goal gave Croatia the lead before Ronaldo converted from the spot to level, and it took a Ramos header deep in stoppage time to settle the matter. Next up is the sternest possible examination: Spain in the Round of 16 on Mon Jul 6 12:00 PT, an Iberian derby in which neither side concedes favorite status lightly, though Portugal's second-place group finish and the late drama against Croatia suggest they are not yet at their sharpest. Survive that and Belgium or the United States await in the quarterfinal, with France a looming possibility further down the road.

Qatar.text: Qatar's group stage exit arrived with little ceremony — a 1-3 defeat to Bosnia & Herzegovina in which they trailed by two goals before half-time, Al-Haydos pulling one back on the stroke of the interval before Mahmic's 80th-minute strike killed any lingering hope of a comeback. The hosts finished bottom of Group B with a single point and a goal difference of minus eight, their tournament a sobering exercise in the gap between ambition and execution at this level.

Saudi Arabia.text: Saudi Arabia's group stage ended with a goalless draw against Cape Verde — a stalemate that was as flat as the scoreline suggests, neither side able to manufacture a breakthrough across ninety minutes. That result, combined with a goal difference of minus five across three matches, proved just enough to squeeze through as one of the best third-placed teams, but the Green Falcons were eliminated in the Round of 32, their tournament ending as quietly as it had begun.

Scotland.text: Brazil ended Scotland's tournament before it had truly begun, a 3-0 defeat that was every bit as one-sided as the scoreline suggests. Júnior struck twice in the first half — the second arriving deep in added time — before Cunha added a third shortly after the interval, leaving Scotland with nothing to build on. They depart having advanced from the group stage as one of the best third-placed sides, a modest distinction that flatters a campaign yielding just one win and four goals conceded. The 2026 World Cup ends for Scotland at the Round of 32.

Senegal.text: Senegal led Belgium twice and carried that advantage deep into extra time, only for Romelu Lukaku to level in the 86th minute and Youri Tielemans to strike twice in three agonising minutes — the second a penalty in stoppage time of extra time — to seal a 3-2 defeat that was as cruel as knockout football gets. The Lions of Teranga had done enough to advance from Group I as one of the best third-placed teams, yet Belgium proved a step too far in the Round of 32. Their tournament ends there: a gutting exit for a side that gave everything and were ultimately undone by a three-minute collapse they could not have scripted in a nightmare.

South Africa.text: South Africa's World Cup ended in the cruelest fashion, a 90th-minute goal from Eustáquio giving Canada a 1-0 win that the balance of the match may not have fully demanded. Bafana Bafana had navigated the group stage with enough grit to finish second in Group A, but a tournament played on home soil — or at least in their confederate hemisphere — ultimately closed in the dying seconds of a Round of 32 tie they very nearly survived.

South Korea.text: South Korea close their World Cup 2026 account with a flat 0-1 defeat to South Africa, Maseko's goal just past the hour mark enough to settle a match the Taeguk Warriors never truly threatened to turn. They had squeezed through the group phase as one of the best third-placed sides, but that slender lifeline ran out in the round of 32 — eliminated without a goal scored in their final two outings, their tournament ending as quietly as it had begun.

Spain.text: Spain dismantled Austria with a composed, near-flawless display in the Round of 32 — Oyarzabal's brace either side of a Porro strike, the second goal coming in the final minute, confirming a clean sheet and total control from first whistle to last. The reward is an Iberian derby against Portugal in the Round of 16 on Mon Jul 6 12:00 PT, a fixture that defies easy prediction: two of European football's most decorated nations, roughly matched in pedigree, with Spain's clinical group-stage form tilting the balance marginally their way but guaranteeing nothing. Survive that and Belgium or the United States await in the quarterfinal, before a potential semifinal collision with France or Morocco that could define the tournament.

Sweden.text: Sweden's tournament ends with a 0-3 defeat to France in the Round of 32 — a verdict that was effectively delivered at half-time, Mbappé's first strike just before the break puncturing whatever hope the Swedes had carried into the tie, and a Barcola goal eight minutes into the second half rendering the contest settled long before his second. They had advanced from Group F as one of the better third-placed sides, a narrow path that always pointed toward exactly this kind of reckoning: a French side operating at a different altitude. The margin was honest.

Switzerland.text: Switzerland closed out the group stage with a purposeful 2-1 win over Canada, striking twice in quick succession either side of the hour mark before absorbing a late consolation — controlled, efficient, and characteristic of a Swiss side that rarely wastes energy on the spectacular. They top Group B having conceded little of substance. Algeria await in the Round of 32 on Thu Jul 2 20:00 PT, a winnable tie on paper but one that demands respect — Algeria have shown at previous tournaments they can suffocate stronger opponents and sting on the break. Should Switzerland advance, Colombia or Ghana could follow, before a potential quarterfinal date with Argentina lurks as the draw's most demanding possibility.

Tunisia.text: Tunisia's group stage exit was written early: two Dutch goals inside seven minutes buried the Carthage Eagles before the match had found its shape, and though Mastouri pulled one back after the break, Hecke's strike shortly after the hour restored the three-goal cushion and sealed a 3-1 defeat. Tunisia finished bottom of Group F without a point, their record of three defeats and a goal difference of minus ten telling the story of a tournament in which they were consistently outclassed — a dispiriting campaign ended against one of the competition's heavyweights.

Turkey.text: Turkey's tournament ends in the cruelest of fashions — a 3-2 win over the United States that arrived too late to matter. They trailed inside three minutes, fought back to lead through Güler and Yilmaz, conceded again early in the second half, and only sealed it through Ayhan's injury-time strike. The drama was real, the effort undeniable, but a fourth-place group finish — even as one of the better third-placed sides — proved an insufficient foundation, and Turkey's World Cup 2026 is over at the group stage.

USA.text: The USA dispatched Bosnia & Herzegovina with a measure of comfort in the Round of 32, Balogun's first-half strike setting the tone before Tillman's late second put the clean sheet beyond doubt. Belgium arrive next in the Round of 16 on Mon Jul 6 17:00 PT — a genuine test of where this American side truly stands, with the Belgians carrying the weight of a storied generation and enough quality to punish any complacency, making this a real contest rather than a formality. Survive that, and a quarterfinal on Fri Jul 10 12:00 PT could bring Portugal or Spain — either encounter a marker of the highest order for a host nation with serious aspirations.

Uruguay.text: Uruguay's group stage ended with a narrow 0-1 defeat to Spain, Álex Baena's first-half strike the solitary difference in a contest that offered the Uruguayans little joy in the final third. Despite advancing as one of the best third-placed sides, their tournament came to an abrupt halt at the same juncture — two draws and a loss across Group H, with a negative goal difference, telling a story of a side that never quite found its footing at this World Cup.

Uzbekistan.text: Uzbekistan's group stage ended with a 1-3 defeat to DR Congo, a result that traced a familiar arc: an encouraging early lead through Shomurodov, then a second-half capitulation as Wissa and Mayele dismantled them after the hour mark, Wissa adding a third in stoppage time to put a harsh gloss on the scoreline. The Central Asians finished bottom of Group K without a point, conceding eleven goals across three matches, and no late reprieve arrived. Their debut on the expanded World Cup stage was brief and bruising, a tournament that exposed the gulf between ambition and readiness at the highest level.
